faq.question: What is a good swimming pace per 100m?
faq.answer: Beginner: 2:30-3:00/100m. Intermediate: 1:45-2:15/100m. Advanced: 1:20-1:45/100m. Competitive: under 1:20/100m. Elite Olympic swimmers race at 0:55-1:05/100m for distance events.

faq.question: How do I improve my swimming pace?
faq.answer: Focus on technique first (streamlining, catch, rotation), then build endurance with interval sets. Drill work improves efficiency more than raw effort. Even 5-10% technique improvement can drop your pace significantly.

faq.question: How does pool length affect pace?
faq.answer: Short course (25m pool) times are typically 1-3% faster than long course (50m pool) due to more wall turns and push-offs. When comparing times, note whether it was short or long course.

faq.question: Is the Swimming Pace Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Swimming Pace Calculator?
sections.body: The Swimming Pace Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your distance, time, time, you get instant results including pace per 100m, pace per 50m, total time. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting pace per 100m right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your distance and need to find the right pace per 100m. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
